mission.title: Install and Configure Wasmcloud on Kubernetes
mission.description: wasmCloud is an open source Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) project that enables teams to build, manage, and scale polyglot Wasm apps across any cloud, K8s, or edge. Installing wasmCloud allows you to leverage its capabilities for creating scalable and portable applications without vendor lock-in.

steps.title: Add the wasmCloud Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official wasmCloud Helm repository to your Helm client using the following command:
```bash
helm repo add wasmcloud https://wasmcloud.github.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install wasmCloud using Helm
steps.description:
Install the wasmCloud chart with the following command, specifying the desired namespace:
```bash
helm install wasmcloud wasmcloud/benchmark --namespace wasmcloud --create-namespace --version 0.2.1
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check the status of the pods to ensure that wasmCloud is running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace wasmcloud
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Edit the Helm release to set resource limits for the wasmCloud deployment. You can do this by updating the values.yaml or using the command:
```bash
helm upgrade wasmcloud wasmcloud/benchmark --namespace wasmcloud --set resources.limits.cpu=500m --set resources.limits.memory=512Mi
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding for access
steps.description:
To access the wasmCloud service, set up port forwarding:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/wasmcloud 8080:80 --namespace wasmcloud
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of wasmCloud will show the pods running in the 'wasmcloud' namespace when you execute `kubectl get pods --namespace wasmcloud`. You will also be able to access the service via port 8080 on your local machine.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add wasmcloud https://wasmcloud.github.io/charts
helm repo update
helm upgrade wasmcloud wasmcloud/benchmark --namespace wasmcloud --set resources.limits.cpu=500m --set resources.limits.memory=512Mi

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
